mechanism_narrative: Rising quarterly real GDP growth combined with expanding nonfarm payrolls indicates broad-based economic expansion, which supports corporate earnings growth and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in expansion periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate crosses above 4.5%, the cost of capital rises sharply, compressing equity valuation multiples and triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 60% of instances where FEDFUNDS exceeds 4.5%, or the signal shows no predictive power in out-of-sample testing over a 3-year period.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous rise in the unemployment rate and deceleration in payroll growth signals labor market deterioration, which historically precedes equity drawdowns and predicts negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict negative returns in more than 50% of triggered instances over a 5-year walk-forward test, or the interaction term coefficient is statistically indistinguishable from zero.

mechanism_narrative: During post-shock recovery phases, an uptick in the industrial production index reflects rebounding manufacturing output and supply chain normalization, providing immediate positive momentum for S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: Industrial production growth shows no correlation with 5-day forward returns during recovery periods, or the signal generates negative returns in > 55% of observations.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in housing starts and retail sales indicates strengthening consumer demand and business investment, which historically correlates with positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The combined housing and retail sales signal predicts negative returns in > 50% of cases over a 3-year rolling window, or the cross-sectional Rank IC drops below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: Once the unemployment rate breaches the 5.0% threshold, it signals a structural shift toward economic contraction, leading to earnings revisions downward and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns remain positive or neutral in > 60% of instances following an UNRATE breach above 5.0%, or the signal loses predictive power after controlling for GDP growth.

mechanism_narrative: When real GDP growth stagnates while the consumer price index accelerates, profit margins face simultaneous demand and cost pressure, predicting negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Equity returns are not significantly lower when GDP growth is below 1.5% and CPI inflation exceeds 3.5%, or the interaction effect vanishes in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: In a zero lower bound environment, further reductions in the federal funds rate signal severe economic distress and limited policy space, which typically triggers negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: The S&P 500 produces positive forward returns in > 55% of instances when FEDFUNDS is near zero, or the signal shows no statistical significance in a 2-year walk-forward evaluation.
